description: Tape Archive (tar) is an incredibly versatile utility used to securely package, compress, and extract multiple files and directories into a single archive.

commands.explanation: Creates (c) an uncompressed archive file containing the specified directory, verbose (v).
Extracts (x) the exact contents of an uncompressed .tar file into the current working directory.
Creates an archive compressed via gzip (z). Fastest compression standard.
Expands and extracts a gzip-compressed (.gz) tar archive.
Creates an archive compressed via bzip2 (j), which yields smaller file sizes than gzip but takes longer.
Creates an archive exclusively compressed via xz (J). Extreme compression ratios for maximum space savings.
Extracts the payload directly into a specified target directory instead of the current working directory.
Lists (t) the strict internal contents of the archive structure without fully extracting the files to disk.
Extracts exclusively one specified matching file out of an entire immense archive.
Packages a directory while explicitly forcing it to ignore/exclude specific files matching a defined pattern.
